rationale: The citation to Spiller et al. (2013) is used for a concrete analytic function, not just background context. The authors explicitly state that Study 4 allows a floodlight test and then run Johnson-Neyman procedures following Spiller et al. to interpret an interaction. They also use the spotlight/floodlight terminology in reporting and figure interpretation. This indicates direct methodological use in the paper's results section.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Spiller et al.'s spotlight/floodlight framework to probe and interpret an interaction effect in Study 4. Specifically, it uses Johnson-Neyman style floodlight testing to identify regions of significance across dietary restraint.
